name: libref-util-xs-perl
title: libref-util-xs-perl
description:
XS implementation for Ref::Util
Ref::Util::XS is the XS implementation of Ref::Util, which provides several
functions to help identify references in a more convenient way than the usual
approach of examining the return value of ref.
.
You should use Ref::Util::XS by installing Ref::Util itself (packaged as
libref-util-perl): If the system doesn't have Recommends turned off, you will
get libref-util-xs-perl automatically. Ref::Util::XS will be used
automatically, providing a significant speed boost to everything that uses
Ref::Util.
.
See Ref::Util for full documentation of the available functions.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use libref-util-xs-perl because XS implementation for Ref::Util.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use libref-util-xs-perl as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair libref-util-xs-perl with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `libref-util-xs-perl --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
